# share.env

> share.env is a hosted SaaS for securely storing and sharing `.env` files
> (API keys, database passwords, and other application secrets) between
> teammates, without pasting them into Slack or email.

Files are owned by a workspace (team), not an individual user. Access is
granted three ways at once: email invite (role-based), an expiring token
link (no login required), and workspace roles (`owner` / `editor` / `viewer`).

## Security

- File contents are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before being written to
  storage — only the ciphertext envelope is ever persisted, never plaintext
  secrets.
- Supabase Storage applies its own default encryption at rest on top of that.
- Row Level Security (RLS) is enabled on every table and storage bucket;
  access is enforced by Postgres policies, not just hidden UI buttons.
- Share links use cryptographically secure, high-entropy tokens, always carry
  a mandatory expiration date, and can be revoked instantly.
- Every change to a file is logged with who made it and when (full audit
  trail).
- share.env is a hosted-only service — self-hosting is not currently
  supported. Client-side zero-knowledge encryption is a deliberate
  non-goal, in favor of a single consistently-enforced server-side security
  model (RLS + AES-256-GCM + audit trail).

## Pricing

Free for up to 3 environments (an environment = one distinct `.env` file per
workspace, e.g. production/staging/preview). $2 per environment per month
after that, billed monthly. Team members are unlimited on every plan.

## Key pages

- [Homepage](https://www.share-env.site/): product overview, security model,
  FAQ.
- [Features](https://www.share-env.site/en/features): encryption, access
  control, audit history.
- [Pricing](https://www.share-env.site/en/pricing): plans and billing FAQ.
- [Use cases](https://www.share-env.site/en/use-cases): onboarding, key
  rotation, offboarding workflows.
- [Security](https://www.share-env.site/en/security): full breakdown of RLS,
  encryption, and vulnerability reporting.
- [CLI](https://www.share-env.site/en/cli): `envshare` CLI for pulling a
  repo's `.env` from the terminal with the same access control as the web
  app.

Polish-language equivalents are available under the `/pl/` locale prefix.
